compiled_state.title: Document sprint and fix-bug as implementations of the Ralph Wiggum autonomous loop pattern
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
Document how DSO's sprint and fix-bug workflows implement the Ralph Wiggum autonomous loop pattern — the dominant community pattern for autonomous AI agent execution. This documentation positions DSO's existing capabilities within the broader ecosystem's vocabulary and helps users and contributors understand DSO's architectural alignment with industry-standard patterns.

## Background: The Ralph Wiggum Pattern

The Ralph Wiggum autonomous loop is the most popular pattern in the Claude Code ecosystem (dedicated subcategory in awesome-claude-code, 5+ projects, Anthropic-cited). It describes an agent that:
- Runs autonomously in a loop until work is complete
- Has circuit breakers to prevent runaway execution
- Includes safety gates that pause for human input at high-stakes decisions
- Manages context window exhaustion through compaction or fresh-context strategies
- Can resume from interruption

## How DSO Already Implements This

### /dso:sprint (Epic Execution Loop)
- **Loop structure:** Phase 3 (batch preparation) → Phase 4 (sub-agent dispatch) → Phase 5 (post-batch validation) → Phase 6 (score check) → back to Phase 3 if incomplete
- **Circuit breakers:** Max 5 concurrent sub-agents; commit+push between batches; 5 cascading failure limit triggers /dso:fix-cascade-recovery
- **Safety gates:** Escalation policy (Autonomous / Escalate-when-blocked / Escalate-unless-confident) set at preplanning; completion verifier dispatched as independent anti-bias check at story/epic closure
- **Context management:** Proactive /compact at 70% context usage; involuntary compaction triggers graceful shutdown; batched test runner prevents timeout
- **Resumability:** CHECKPOINT 1/6-6/6 protocol on sub-agents; ticket comments preserve state; --resume flag reads checkpoints to fast-close near-complete tasks

### /dso:fix-bug (Investigation-Fix Loop)
- **Loop structure:** Classify → Investigate (tiered: BASIC→INTERMEDIATE→ADVANCED→ESCALATED) → Hypothesis validation gate → RED test → Fix → Verify
- **Circuit breakers:** 3-strike escalation on investigation failure; RED-before-fix gate blocks code changes without failing test; max_fix_validate_cycles config bound
- **Safety gates:** Intent search gate (is this intentional behavior?); reversal check (does the fix undo a prior decision?); user approval of root cause before implementation
- **Context management:** Investigation dispatched to sub-agents (fresh context); intent search budget capped at configurable tool calls
- **Resumability:** Bug ticket comments record investigation findings; CHECKPOINT protocol on investigation sub-agents

### /dso:debug-everything (Health Restoration Loop)
- **Loop structure:** Bug-Fix Mode (iterate through open bugs) or Diagnostic Mode (scan → triage → fix pipeline); inner fix→validate loop bounded by debug.max_fix_validate_cycles
- **Circuit breakers:** Session lock prevents concurrent debug sessions; oscillation guard detects ping-pong between fix attempts; file-overlap detection for conflicting agent edits
- **Safety gates:** Safeguard analysis flags bugs in protected files; interactivity mode defers blocking gates as machine-parseable comments in non-interactive mode
- **Resumability:** Session lock + epic ticket state; graceful shutdown with summary note

## Documentation Deliverables

1. **Architecture doc** — plugins/dso/docs/AUTONOMOUS-LOOP-ARCHITECTURE.md mapping DSO's loop patterns to the Ralph Wiggum vocabulary (loop structure, circuit breakers, safety gates, context management, resumability)
2. **README update** — reference the pattern in the project README so ecosystem users recognize DSO's approach
3. **Comparison table** — DSO vs. ralph-orchestrator vs. GSD vs. ECC loop implementations, highlighting where DSO is more comprehensive (two-layer enforcement, sub-agent isolation, anti-bias completion verification)
4. **Differentiator documentation** — explicitly call out what DSO adds beyond the basic pattern:
   - Two-layer defense-in-depth (git hooks + tool-use hooks) — most Ralph implementations have soft gates only
   - Anti-bias completion verification via independent sub-agent — no other implementation does this
   - Event-sourced ticket system as inter-agent coordination bus — most use file-based state
   - TDD enforcement with RED markers — most have optional testing
   - Tiered model escalation (haiku→sonnet→opus) — most use a single model
